description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a rectangular footprint and a sloped, gray roof featuring several chimneys and antennas. The facade is finished in a muted beige color with visible weathering, and the building has four stories above ground level. Rows of uniformly spaced windows line each floor, with balconies present on the longer sides, each protected by simple railings. The ground floor features two main entrances with dark red doors, symmetrically placed. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation, reflecting a practical architectural approach common in many urban residential neighborhoods.
description: A four-story beige apartment building with a sloped gray roof, balconies on the long sides, and two main entrances.
description_view_0: The short side of the building is visible, showing four stories of windows but no balconies or entrances.
description_view_1: The long side of the building is shown, featuring rows of balconies, two main entrances, and many windows.
description_view_2: The opposite short side is visible, mirroring the first with only windows and no balconies or doors.
description_view_3: The second long side is shown, similar to view 1, with balconies and windows but no visible entrances.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in evenly spaced vertical and horizontal rows on all sides, with each floor having a consistent pattern.
